title: The Cabin in the Field
surface: A man is walking through a remote field when he discovers a small cabin. Inside the cabin are two skeletons. The cabin is not made of wood. What happened?
truth: The 'cabin' is the cockpit of a small aeroplane that crashed in the field. The two skeletons are those of the pilot and co-pilot. The plane went down in this remote area and was never found until now. The cockpit — made of metal — became the 'cabin' the man stumbled upon.
key_facts: The cabin is the cockpit of a crashed aircraft
The two skeletons are the pilot and co-pilot
The aircraft crashed in the remote field and was never discovered
The cabin is made of metal, not wood
The wreckage remained in the field for a long time

clues.title: Made of Metal
clues.content: The cabin walls are made of metal, riveted together in sections. The interior is cramped with instrument panels — now rusted and broken — and two seats bolted to the floor.

clues.title: Wreckage
clues.content: Scattered around the cabin are fragments of a larger structure — twisted metal, broken glass, and what appears to be a wing partially buried in the earth.

clues.title: Two Seats
clues.content: The interior has exactly two seats, side by side, with harnesses still buckled. Each seat contains a skeleton. There is no room for passengers — this structure was only designed for two.

hints: What kind of cabin is not made of wood?
How might two people end up inside a metal cabin in the middle of a field?
Think about structures that travel — and sometimes do not arrive.
The cabin came to the field. It did not grow there.
